title: Convert Date to 5 Digit Number
excerpt:
Hi there, Monday Community! 
Hopefully this is possible…in excel you can take a date and change it into a 5 digit number by clicking on Format Cells and clicking on general within the numbers tab.  Is there a way in Mond…

posts.author: Chrystle (Chrystle)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, Monday Community!

Hopefully this is possible…in excel you can take a date and change it into a 5 digit number by clicking on Format Cells and clicking on general within the numbers tab. Is there a way in Monday.com to turn a date into a 5 digit number as well?

Thanks in advance!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Chrystle

I’m curious why you would want to do that.

In any case, this will work:

DATEVALUE({Date})

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man
Watch Our Latest Video: A Killer Combo: monday.com, Integromat & Google Sheets - Recurring Tasks Example

posts.author: Chrystle (Chrystle)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much!

I’m building out a billing formula. For one of our services, we charge by the day, I want to be able to have my team input the date the client started their service and it will calculate how much to bill per quarter. The downside to doing it by date is that the formula could get quite complex as the years roll on unless I created separate boards…Not wanting to do that. My other solution is to stick with manually inputting a number that corresponds with the date, example: January 1 = 365, December 31=1. This would allow me to keep the same formula (with the exception of leap years) and keep the years on the same board.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Chrystle

You might want to check out the DAYS() or WORKDAYS() functions as well.

posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
